name: Tamam Shud Cipher
discovery_location: Back cover of Rubaiyat of Omar Khayyam (1941 Whitcombe & Tombs edition)
detection_method: Infrared photography of faint pencil indentations
connected_to: Unknown man (later identified as Carl Webb) found dead at Somerton Park beach, Adelaide, South Australia

L2.note: Struck through by writer; shares MLIA prefix with L5

L4.note: Single letter on its own line; likely a volta/separator between stanza halves

notes: First letter of L1 debated as W or M (W most accepted)
L2 is crossed out; appears to be a draft of L5
An X mark may also appear above the last O in L5 in some readings
Revealed by infrared photography of pencil indentations on book cover

edition.note: Rare wartime edition; last page torn out (Tamam Shud slip found on body)
